title: Design Researcher — Litigant Experience
summary: Interview litigants, lawyers, and court staff to map the real experience of navigating an Indian court — and turn it into insights the ecosystem can act on.
html:
About the role

PUCAR's work starts from the litigant's lived experience — the waits, the commutes, the adjournments. We're looking for a design researcher to help us document that experience rigorously, so that every initiative we support is grounded in reality rather than assumption.

[placeholder description — refine with the actual scope before publishing]

What you'll do

  • Plan and run interviews with litigants, lawyers, and court staff
  • Synthesise findings into journey maps and insight briefs
  • Work with the initiatives teams to turn insights into design principles

Who this suits

  • 2+ years in design research, service design, or adjacent fields
  • Comfort conducting interviews in at least one Indian language besides English
  • Curiosity about public systems; no legal background required

How it works

This is a volunteer engagement with a PUCAR collective member organisation. We'll set you up with context, introductions, and a buddy from the network.

title: Open-Source Contributor — Court Data Dashboards
summary: Help build open dashboards that make court performance legible — pendency, disposal rates, hearing patterns — on top of public judicial data.
html:
About the role

Measuring court performance is one of PUCAR's core initiatives. The tooling is open source, and we welcome contributors who can take issues end-to-end.

[placeholder description — refine with the actual scope before publishing]

What you'll do

  • Pick up issues on the public repos (data pipelines, visualisation, docs)
  • Help design metrics that reflect the litigant's experience, not just throughput
  • Review contributions from other volunteers

Who this suits

  • Comfortable with Python or JavaScript and messy public data
  • Experience with open-source workflows (issues, PRs, reviews)
  • Interest in justice-system data; domain experts especially welcome

How it works

Start from the GitHub org, introduce yourself, and pick a good-first-issue. Regular contributors are invited into the initiative's working group.

title: Legal Process Fellow — People-Centric Courts
summary: Work with a partner court to redesign one high-friction process — summons, cause lists, or adjournments — from the litigant's point of view.
html:
About the fellowship

The People-Centric Courts initiative embeds fellows with partner courts to redesign everyday processes that create the most friction for litigants.

[placeholder description — refine with the actual scope before publishing]

What you'll do

  • Shadow one process end-to-end and document where time and trust are lost
  • Co-design improvements with judges, registry staff, and lawyers
  • Publish the playbook openly so other courts can adopt it

Who this suits

  • Law graduates or practitioners with 1–5 years of experience
  • Strong writing; patience for institutional change
  • Based in, or willing to relocate to, the partner court's city

How it works

Fellowships are stipended through a PUCAR collective member organisation. Cohorts start quarterly.
